audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for burning bush, holy ground, Divine seeing/hearing, YHWH name disclosure, liberation commission.

verses.restored: Moses was shepherding the flock of Jethro his father-in-law, priest of Midian; he led the flock beyond the wilderness and came to mountain of the Divine, to Horeb.
Messenger of YHWH appeared to him in flame of fire from midst of the bush; he saw, and look, the bush burned with fire, yet the bush was not consumed.
Moses said, Let me turn aside and see this great sight, why the bush is not burned up.
YHWH saw that he turned aside to see, and Divine called to him from midst of the bush, saying, Moses, Moses. He said, Here I am.
He said, Do not come near here. Take your sandals from your feet, for the place on which you stand is holy ground.
He said, I am the Divine of your father, Divine of Abraham, Divine of Isaac, and Divine of Jacob. Moses hid his face, for he feared to look toward the Divine.
YHWH said, Seeing, I have seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and I have heard their cry because of their taskmasters; for I know their pains.

I have come down to rescue them from hand of Egypt and to bring them up from that land to a good and spacious land, to a land flowing with milk and honey, to place of Canaanite, Hittite, Amorite, Perizzite, Hivite, and Jebusite.
Now look, the cry of the children of Israel has come to me; and I have also seen the oppression with which Egypt oppresses them.
And now go, and I will send you to Pharaoh, so that you may bring my people, children of Israel, out from Egypt.
Moses said to the Divine, Who am I, that I should go to Pharaoh and bring the children of Israel out from Egypt?
He said, Because I will be with you. And this shall be sign for you that I myself sent you: when you bring the people out from Egypt, you shall serve/worship the Divine upon this mountain.
Moses said to the Divine, Look, I come to the children of Israel and say to them, The Divine of your fathers has sent me to you, and they say to me, What is his name? What shall I say to them?
Divine said to Moses, I AM / I WILL BE WHO I AM / I WILL BE. And he said, Thus you shall say to the children of Israel: I AM / I WILL BE has sent me to you.

Divine said further to Moses, Thus you shall say to the children of Israel: YHWH, Divine of your fathers, Divine of Abraham, Divine of Isaac, and Divine of Jacob, has sent me to you. This is my name into the age, and this is my memorial from generation to generation.

Go and gather elders of Israel, and say to them, YHWH, Divine of your fathers, appeared to me, Divine of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying, Visiting, I have visited you and what is done to you in Egypt.
And I said, I will bring you up from affliction of Egypt to land of Canaanite, Hittite, Amorite, Perizzite, Hivite, and Jebusite, to land flowing with milk and honey.

They will hear your voice, and you and elders of Israel shall come to king of Egypt and say to him: YHWH, Divine of Hebrews, has met with us; and now please let us go a journey of three days in wilderness, so we may sacrifice to YHWH our Divine.
But I know that king of Egypt will not give you leave to go, not even by strong hand.
So I will send my hand and strike Egypt with all my wonders that I will do in its midst; afterward he will send you away.
And I will give this people favor in eyes of Egypt; when you go, you will not go empty.

Each woman shall ask from her neighbor and from the one sojourning in her house objects of silver and objects of gold and garments; you shall put them upon your sons and daughters, and you shall plunder Egypt.

translation_notes: YHWH name disclosure is preserved with Hebrew verbal ambiguity: I AM / I WILL BE.
Holy ground appears in wilderness, outside imperial and settled sacred control.
Divine response to oppression is seeing, hearing, knowing, coming down, rescuing, and sending.
Avad shifts toward serving/worshiping YHWH rather than Pharaoh.
safety_note: The promise of land names existing peoples; later conquest material requires careful ethical audit. Here the focus is liberation from oppression and Divine presence with the sent one.
